steps.title: Identify the shared diagnosis
steps.content:
The key signal is that both texts describe scholarship as presenting an incomplete vision of the period.

Text 1 shows this through overrepresentation of experimental fragmentation, which obscures how the period actually looked.

Text 2 shows this through scholars' narrow focus on the fragmentation-versus-coherence binary, which “inhibits inquiry” into other stylistic patterns.

Both identify the same underlying problem: current scholarship cannot see the full picture.

steps.title: Match to the shared agreement
steps.content:
B states the joint conclusion: without a shift in focus, scholarship's vision will continue to be unnecessarily limited.

Text 1 agrees—neglecting coherent narratives perpetuates a distorted picture.

Text 2 agrees—limiting inquiry to fragmentation versus coherence obscures other stylistic patterns.

A wrongly calls for moving beyond style; C and D misread the problems.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
